description:
## What
`tests/common/mod.rs` with a `spawn_test_app()` function returning a `TestApp` struct. The struct holds a randomly-bound TCP port, an in-memory SQLite database (WAL mode, all required PRAGMAs), a base URL, and an admin bearer token. Every integration test in `tests/` must use this harness — no ad-hoc server setup, no mocking of the database layer.

## Why
Without a shared harness, every test file reinvents server startup, leading to divergence, test pollution, and flaky port conflicts. A single canonical harness ensures all integration tests run against the same code paths as production.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: read axum integration test patterns from the axum docs and examples repo; identify how `axum::serve` binds to port 0 and how to extract the bound address; check how `sqlx::SqlitePool` with `mode=memory&cache=shared` can be seeded with migrations at test startup.
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: write `tests/common/mod.rs` with `spawn_test_app()` that calls `sqlx::migrate!()`, creates an admin persona row, issues a test OAuth token, spawns the axum app on port 0, and returns `TestApp { base_url, token, db }`. Wire it into one smoke-test to prove the harness works.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write at least two integration tests that call `spawn_test_app()` and assert: (a) GET /api/v1/instance returns 200 with a valid JSON body; (b) GET /.well-known/webfinger?resource=acct:writer@localhost returns 200 with a valid JRD JSON body.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: axum integration test patterns from https://github.com/tokio-rs/axum/tree/main/examples and axum docs "Testing" section.
- Test approach: real SQLite in-memory with migrations applied; real HTTP via `reqwest`; assert on HTTP status codes and response body shape (not implementation internals).

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths — `spawn_test_app()` may use `unwrap()` only inside the test helper itself, not in any production code path it exercises.
- Port 0 binding must be used (never hardcode a test port).
- Admin token must be a valid OAuth bearer token that passes the production auth middleware.
- Migration failures must panic loudly with context, not silently skip.
- `TestApp` must implement `Drop` to shut down the background tokio task and close the DB pool.
title: Integration test harness setup
